hypothesis: This experiment tests whether a LOSS AVERSION frame ('Stop Losing Sales') converts better than the current ENABLEMENT frame ('Let Your Customers Order Anytime') because loss aversion is ~2x more motivating than equivalent gains (Kahneman/Tversky), and these vendors already feel the pain of customers asking to order between markets — naming that lost revenue should create urgency to act. Secondary test: price-anchored CTA ('Get Your $10/mo Store') vs action-generic CTA ('Start Selling Today') to reduce perceived risk at the click moment.

challenger_copy.hero_subheadline: Your regulars already want to order online. A simple $10/mo storefront — live in 15 minutes.
challenger_copy.mid_page_hook: Every market day ends. Your sales don't have to.
